findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphicProdMap.lean:166-169` hard-codes `(s ×ˢ t)`; Mathlib’s underlying theorem is already general: `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/FDeriv/Prod.lean:312-315` has `HasFDerivWithinAt.prodMap {s : Set (E × G)}` with coordinate-image source sets.
findings.fix: Replace or add a general lemma with signature `hf : IsJHolomorphicWithinAt J₁ K₁ f (Prod.fst '' u) p.1`, `hg : IsJHolomorphicWithinAt J₂ K₂ g (Prod.snd '' u) p.2` proving holomorphicity within `u`, and derive the rectangular version as a corollary if desired.
findings.issue: `IsJHolomorphicWithinAt.prodMap` only proves the product map within `s ×ˢ t`; the natural `WithinAt` form should work over an arbitrary `u : Set (V × W)` using hypotheses on `Prod.fst '' u` and `Prod.snd '' u`.

summary: The coordinate-restriction generality issue is fixed, but the within-set product-map lemma is still below the natural derivative-level API: it hard-codes rectangular domains where Mathlib supports arbitrary product-source subsets.
